title: Use Cases & Actors — Capturing What the System Does

sections.heading: What is a Use Case?
Actors and System Boundaries
Include and Extend relationships
sections.content:
<>: Mandatory sub-behavior (always happens) <>: Optional/conditional behavior 
// Example: Place Order
// <> Validate Payment  ← always happens
// <> Check Inventory   ← always happens
// <>  Apply Coupon      ← only if coupon provided
// <>  Gift Wrap         ← only if requested

// In Spring Boot terms:
// include = method call in the main service flow
// extend = conditional logic (if/feature flag)

@Service
public class OrderService {
    public Order place(OrderRequest req) {
        validate(req);                        // <>
        checkInventory(req.getItems());       // <>
        if (req.getCoupon() != null) {        // <>
            applyCoupon(req);
        }
        return createOrder(req);
    }
}

sections.heading: When to use class diagrams
Relationships and multiplicity

title: Sequence Diagrams — Showing How Objects Interact

sections.heading: Reading and creating sequence diagrams
Async and alt fragments
sections.content:
// Async messaging (Kafka/JMS):
//
// Service          Kafka           Consumer
//    │               │               │
//    │──publish()───▶│               │
//    │◀──ack─────────│               │
//    │               │──deliver()───▶│  (async, later)
//    │               │               │──process()
//    │               │◀──commit──────│

// ALT fragment (conditional logic):
//
// Client        Service        PaymentGW
//    │             │               │
//    │──pay()────▶│               │
//    │             │──charge()───▶│
//    │             │◀─────────────│
//    │             │               │
//    ┌─── alt ────────────────────────┐
//    │ [success]   │               │  │
//    │  │◀─200─────│               │  │
//    ├─── else ───────────────────────┤
//    │ [failed]    │               │  │
//    │  │◀─402─────│               │  │
//    └────────────────────────────────┘
Interview use: Draw sequence diagram when asked "Walk me through what happens when..." — shows you can decompose complex flows into clear interactions.
